items.name: Escape the Nautiloid
items.description: Start quest Escape the Nautiloid .

items.name: Us
items.description: Befriend Us . If he survives you will receive the ability Summon Us during Act Two.

items.name: Rune Slate
items.description: Read the Rune Slate in the first room, where you start. You will have an Advantage on Intelligence check during quest Help Omeluum Investigate the Parasite .

items.name: Dark Mind
items.description: Take Dark Mind . You will be able to read it in the Mindflayer Colony later in Act 2 by using Mind-Archive Interface and Slack-Skinned Head .

items.name: Slave Mind
items.description: Take Slave Mind . You will be able to read it in the Mindflayer Colony later in Act 2 by using Mind-Archive Interface and Slack-Skinned Head .

items.name: Everburn Blade
items.description: Obtain Everburn Blade . Use Command: Drop ​ on Commander Zhalk (Lv. 8) and pick up the sword.

tooltip.lore: Silent flames surge the length of the blade as soon as it clears the scabbard.

items.name: Daughter of Darkness
items.description: Start Shadowheart companion quest

items.name: The Pale Elf
items.description: Start the Astarion companion quest.

items.name: Shadowheart
items.description: Recruit the Half-Elf companion.

items.name: Astarion
items.description: Recruit the High-Elf companion

items.name: The Wizard of Waterdeep
items.description: Start The Wizard of Waterdeep companion quest.

items.name: Free Lae'zel
items.description: Start Free Lae'zel companion quest.

items.name: Silver Pendant
items.description: Collect the Silver Pendant from the outpost.

tooltip.lore: Grime covers this necklace, but you can vaguely discern delicate engravings of a moon and harp.

items.name: Spider Egg Sac
items.description: Interact with the Rocky Crevice to receive the Spider Egg Sac .

items.name: Gale
items.description: Recruit Gale .

items.name: Lae'zel
items.description: Recruit Lae'zel .

items.name: Adventuring Party (Lv. 2) Encounter
items.description: Handle the Adventuring Party encounter.

items.name: Explore the Ruins
items.description: Start Explore the Ruins side-quest.

items.name: Bandits (Lv. 2) Encounter
items.description: Defeat the Bandit encounter.

items.name: Book of Dead Gods
items.description: Read Book of Dead Gods .

items.name: Soul Coin
items.description: Take the Soul Coin in Dank Crypt trapped Sarcophagus room.

items.name: Soul Coin
items.description: The coin is inside the sarcophagus near the Book of Dead Gods

items.name: Soul Coin
items.description: Take the Soul Coin in the sarcophagus where you find Withers .

items.name: The Amulet of Lost Voices
items.description: Looted from the Dank Crypt chest.

tooltip.lore: The dead hold no secrets from Jergal's Scriveners of Doom.

items.name: The Watcher's Guide
items.description: Looted from a sarcophagus in Dank Crypt.

tooltip.lore: Time has dampened this spear's sheen, but the centre glows a faint blue. The magic it exudes feels old, terrible... and divine. The spear was given to its previous owner for his unerring loyalty - even after death.

items.name: Goblin Raid
items.description: Defeat the Goblin Raid . Protect Berth and Aradin . If Berth survives, you will be able to do the quest Return the Locket .

items.name: Return the Locket
items.description: Find the stolen locket. Available only if Berth survives the Goblin Raid (Lv. 2)

items.name: Gloves of Power
items.description: Obtained near the entrance to Emerald Grove, after you kill Za'Krug. One of the best stealing gloves

tooltip.lore: Blessed by Priestess Gut, these gauntlets provide additional aid to any who bear the Absolute's mark upon their flesh.

items.name: Bugbear Assassin (Lv. 3) Encounter
items.description: Save Nadira from the Bugbear Assassin .

items.name: Soul Coin
items.description:
• You can convince Nadira to give it to you

• Can be stolen from her

• If the Bugbear Assassin killed her, just loot it from her.

items.name: Heal Pandirna
items.description: Pandirna is paralysed, you can use Lesser Restoration ​ or Amulet of Silvanus .

items.name: Recruit Wyll
items.description: Recruit the Human companion to your party.

items.name: Speak with the Strange Ox
items.description: Use Speak with Animals to speak with the Strange Ox.​

items.name: Shapeshifter's Boon Ring
items.description: I would suggest avoiding picking this item, as it requires killing the Strange Ox . If you kill it in Act 1, you will not be able to obtain Hat of Fire Acuity . However, there is a way to do it peacefully. Click on the Shapeshifter's Boon Ring , for instructions on the peaceful way to obtain it in Act 1.

tooltip.lore: 'I'm a talented shapeshifter, too. I can shapeshift a thin body into a fat body. All I need is a steady supply of mince pies and mulled wine.' - Overheard in a tavern in Daggerford

items.name: Perform with Alfira
items.description: Speak with Alfira and perform with her, you will receive a Lute , gain proficiency in musical instruments and a permanent ability to cast Bardic Inspiration .​

items.name: Save Arabella
items.description:
Complete the quest Save Arabella . You will receive Komira's Locket as a reward.

Note: Complete this quest before Save The Refugees for additional rewards.

tooltip.lore: Worked carefully into the locket's metal back are the words, 'Never dark again.'

items.name: Investigate the Beach
items.description:
Complete the quest Investigate the Beach .

Note: Complete this quest before Save The Refugees for additional rewards.

tooltip.lore: When the light hits it just so, the centre jewel shimmers with every colour of the rainbow.

items.name: Investigate Kagha
items.description:
Complete the quest Investigate Kagha . Player can receive these rewards:

• Broodmother's Revenge - if Kagha was knocked out or killed the party can loot it from her.

• Pale Oak - if Kagha survives and the player is Druid.

• Hellrider's Pride

Note: complete this quest before Rescue the Druid Halsin .

items.name: Broodmother's Revenge
items.description: One of more rarer amulets as it gives direct damage increase.

tooltip.lore: Each of this amulet's jewels shines like a viper's eyes.
Infused with Silvanus' strength, these staves are gifted to Faithwardens who act in defence of nature.

items.name: Hellrider's Pride
items.description: Perfect item for support builds that use spells like Mass Healing Word ​ and Healing Word .

tooltip.lore: A waft of sulphur emanates from these proudly-kept gauntlets.

items.name: Steal the Sacred Idol
items.description:
Complete the quest Steal the Sacred Idol . The party will receive Ring of Protection as a reward.

Note: complete this quest after Investigate Kagha and before Save The Refugees .

items.name: Ring of Protection
items.description: Due to its defensive bonuses, the ring is useful throughout the whole game.

tooltip.lore: Elegant motifs of warding - shields, targes, bucklers, and buffers - are faintly impressed into the metal round of the ring.

items.name: Save the Refugees
items.description: Complete Save the Refugees quest. If you want to receive Wapira's Crown you will have to accept the reward that will be offered after you complete the quest.

tooltip.lore: Legends say a giant elk and a druid once formed a powerful friendship. When the elk laid down his life for the druid, she crafted this helmet in honour of her beloved friend, so his beauty and compassion would live on forever.

items.name: Rescue the Druid Halsin
items.description: Complete Rescue the Druid Halsin . After completing the quest speak with Rath to gain access to the Hidden Vault . Where you can obtain Sorrow and Robe of Summer .

tooltip.lore: For every drop of blood spilled, a piece of the soul perishes - until nothing remains but regret.
This robe's thin fabric is pleasantly warm to the touch.
This amulet, engraved with a small waterfall, imparts a feeling of calm serenity when worn.

items.name: Cap of Curing
items.description: The cap is relevant for the Bard class, albeit the bonus healing is minor.

tooltip.lore: Putting on this hat is somehow enough to lift your spirits.

items.name: Mind Flayer Parasite Specimen
items.description: Located inside the Enclave Library .

tooltip.lore: Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.

items.name: Save the Goblin Sazza
items.description: Start the quest after finishing Investigate Kagha and before finishing Defeat the Goblins .

tooltip.lore: Somehow, this axe perpetually feels warm to the touch.
These gloves seem to meld to your skin - supple, snug and dextrous as one could hope for.
This simple cured goat hide was enchanted by a travelling druid. A goatherd once helped her negotiate a strange maze of hedges, so she blessed all his goats in thanks.
A pale staff inlaid with droplet-shaped topaz. It is cool to the touch.

items.name: Ring of Flinging
items.description: Great ring for Thrower builds.

tooltip.lore: Organisations like the Harpers do not relegate their activities to head-to-head combat - many a moonlit chase with an evildoer has been resolved with a loose roof tile and a decent throwing arm.
A shiver of magic flows from this bow into your hand - like a promise.

items.name: Hunting Shortbow
items.description: A decent bow, that can be useful in Act 1, as there are multiple monstrosity-type enemies.

tooltip.lore: Supple and strong, this shortbow has been imbued with a ranger's solemn magic - running your fingers along the smooth ash, you can feel that solemnity like a ghost in the shaped wood.

items.name: Safeguard Shield
items.description: Good shield for the early game due to its saving throw bonus

tooltip.lore: Magic got caught in this heirloom by being exposed to too many wizard family dinners.
This light, flexible staff is of elven make, marked with mithral sigils that channel magic in the blink of an eye.

items.name: The Whispering Promise
items.description: One of the best rings for Support builds that use spells like Healing Word ​ or Mass Healing Word .

tooltip.lore: A soft, soothing voice floats from this ring, reciting a prayer over and over.

The branches of this circlet are half-fossilised, passed down through generations of druids. Fabricated during the founding of the grove, the wreath is forever linked to its lands, the tiny rubies connected to its stones and structures, bidding them to do their bidding.
Thread-like vines wriggle along this wooden staff.

items.name: Shaft of a Broken Spear
items.description: Pick from Edowin's Body. It will be used to create the Vision of the Absolute .

items.name: Mind Flayer Parasite Specimen
items.description: Can be looted from Edowin

tooltip.lore: Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.
Plain leather with a faint but unmistakable buzz - as if eager to put idle hands to work.
Embroidered with a simple maxim - 'Nature is the True State of the World' - these hides radiate a faint divine power.

items.name: Head of a Broken Spear
items.description: Merge it with the Shaft of a Broken Spear , to create Vision of the Absolute .

items.name: Vision of the Absolute
items.description: Crafter from Head of a Broken Spear and Shaft of a broken spear

tooltip.lore: The tip of this spear strikes unerringly towards its target's eyes.

items.name: Moondrop Pendant
items.description: Inside Selune sealed chest.

tooltip.lore: The back bears an inscription under a delicate crescent moon. 'You're a novice no more, Robin. Only right you should carry a little of our Lady's light with you.'

items.name: Owlbear Cub
items.description: Spare the Owlbear cub.

items.name: Selûnite Rite
items.description:
The rite is found inside a Gilded chest.

By reading the rite you will have an easier check to gain a Nightsong point after Shadowheart has her flashback about the black wolf.

items.name: Scratch the Dog
items.description: Recruit the dog that is protecting his dead master.

items.name: Goblin Encounters
items.description: Defeat the goblins in the village.

tooltip.lore: The helm throbs when violence is near, filling the wearer with a compulsion to act.

items.name: Finish the Masterwork Weapon
items.description: Craft one of the masterwork weapons.

tooltip.lore: Smoke-like whorls of sussur sap darken the metal of this dagger's blade and silence its victims.
Sussur roots extend from this sword's hilt into the blade itself. It yearns to drink magic, silencing every creature it strikes.
The sheen of sussur sap forged into this sickle glows blue, silencing every creature it cuts.
It is said the Speedy Lightfeet were the first of many lightning inventions made by Yrre the Sparkstruck. The boots' old, frayed edges zap and crackle with even the slightest movement.

items.name: Decide fate of the gnome.
items.description: Decide what happens with the gnome that is captured by the goblins.

tooltip.lore: It's really very heavy.

items.name: Ogres in Blighted Village
items.description:
Deal with the ogres. There are two available outcomes:

• Recruit the ogres - you will be able to summon them with Lump's War Horn .

• Kill the ogres - you will be able to loot Warped Headband of Intellect .

items.name: Warped Headband of Intellect
items.description: Try to at least recruit the Ogres, so that you could obtain the time you needed it.

tooltip.lore: This particular circlet is covered in yellow slime and has pieces of half-digested mutton stuck behind the gems.
Though the grip's leather is old and crusty, it shows no signs of wear, and not a single scratch mars the slender blade.

items.name: Explore the Apothecary's Cellar
items.description: You will find some unique interactions, items and an encounter here.

tooltip.lore: Delicate twists of magical iron - painstakingly gilded - have been fashioned into these fortifying vambraces.

items.name: Necromancy of Thay
items.description:
You can either destroy or read the book. If you successfully read it:

• You will gain a ritual Speak with Dead ​ spell if you read atleast one page.

• Turning three pages you will gain Forbbiden Knowledge which grants +1 on Wisdom saving thows.

items.name: Scroll of Summon Quasit
items.description:
You can gain a new companion named Shovel:

•
By scribing the scroll with a Wizard.

•
Use the scroll to summon it. Important: the member who is summonging must not have subclass Storm Sorcery, Wild Magic or Shadow Magic.

•
Summoning him after finishing Act 1.

items.name: Dark Amethyst
items.description: Required to read Necromancy of Thay .

tooltip.lore: Found in the Spider Queen's stomach, this robe emanates a sour smell and stings to the touch.
Slightly sticky to the touch, these boots have excellent grip.
Tiny barbs prick the wearer when they're injured, as if urging flight from danger.

items.name: Hunt the Devil
items.description: Help paladins kill the person they are hunting.

items.name: Sword of Justice
items.description: Obtained from Anders.

tooltip.lore: The words 'Deliverance. Justice. Vengeance.' are engraved on the base of this sword. They echo in your mind.

items.name: Soul Coin
items.description: Located on the roof of the tollhouse.

items.name: Recruit Karlach
items.description: Recruit the Tiefling.

items.name: Our Fiery Friend
items.description: Starts as soon as you recruit Karlach.

items.name: The Hellion's Heart
items.description: Starts as a sub-quest of Our Fiery Friend . You will need to bring Infernal Iron to Dammon to upgrade Karlach's heart.

items.name: Smuggler's Ring
items.description: One of the best stealing ring in the game.

tooltip.lore: A subtle accessory prized by purveyors of the covert arts.

items.name: Find the Missing Shipment
items.description:
Help find the missing shipment.

Note: if you will want to successfuly complete the quest either Olly and Rugan have to bring it back or you will have to bring the whole chest without looking inside or unlocking it.

items.name: Flind and Gnolls encounter
items.description: Defeat the gnolls.

items.name: Mind Flayer Parasite Specimen
items.description: Can be looted from Flind .

tooltip.lore: Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.

items.name: Speedy Reply
items.description: Can be looted from a dead caravan agent.

tooltip.lore: A heavy haft and wide blade belie the speed of this weapon. It parts the air as swiftly as any duelling sabre.

items.name: Shattered Flail
items.description: Can be looted from Flind.

tooltip.lore: A bundle of bones and blood - and a soul-piercing demonic presence - is all that remains of the Flind's flail. It would make a decent mace with a particularly nasty bite.

items.name: Gain Password To Enter the Thief Hideout
items.description: Save Rugan or use Speak with Dead ​ to get the password to enter Zhentarim Hideout .

items.name: Reason's Grasp
items.description: Found inside a trapped and locked wooden chest inside the cave.

tooltip.lore: The snug fit of these gloves resist a balled fist - as if favouring the open hand of peace.

items.name: Gloves of Heroism
items.description: Located in the tollhouse basement.

tooltip.lore: Suede covers the palms of these gloves - ensuring a soft, comforting touch for anyone in need of help.

items.name: Rescue the Grand Duke
items.description: To start the quest speak with the Flaming Fist soldier at Waukeen's Rest

items.name: Rescue the Trapped Man
items.description: Save the man stuck upstairs.

items.name: Hamarhraft
items.description: Found upstairs inside a guilded chest.

tooltip.lore: The Dwarven grammar is garbled, but the weapon's name suggests something about the importance of first impressions.

items.name: Svartlebee's Woundseeker
items.description:
Carried by Gauntlet Yeva . It is possible to acquire it without killing her by using Command: Drop ​ and then use a spell like Darkness ​ on the sword in order to steal it.

One of the best two-handed weapons in Act 1.

tooltip.lore: Wielded by Svartlebee The Custodian, so named because he had the following reputation: Have you got someone in your life causing a mess? Send Svartlebee a missive. He shall clean them up, and then clean up the crime scene.

items.name: The Sparky Points
items.description:
If you save Counsellor Florrick you will have a choice to receive one of these weapons as a reward.

​

tooltip.lore: When Yrre the Sparkstruck was asked why they'd created a trident twice their size, they simply replied, 'The question is why not?'
Sleek and lightweight, it is said that the Joltshooter was Yrre the Sparkstruck's favourite weapon. They were indifferent towards bows, but the spikes jutting out of it?

Yrre the Sparkstruck intended to give the Spellsparkler to Lenore, a cleric of Mystra and old lover. But when the gnome returned to the cleric's tower, they found it empty - inhabited only by dust and abandoned experiments.

items.name: Speak With the Frightened Ox
items.description: For some unique dialogue you can use Speak with Animals to talk with it.​

items.name: Enter Zhentarim Hideout
items.description: Gain entrance to Zhentarim Hideout .

items.name: Free the Artist
items.description: Start Free the Artist quest.

tooltip.lore: Named after Harold Butterbisk, who owned the Butterbisk Inn in Waterdeep before it was renamed by new owners. The stock of the crossbow reads: Quaffing is a shootable offence.

items.name: Abyss Beckoners
items.description: Can be stolen, located inside an ornate chest.

tooltip.lore: Demons will bite their masters, slaves, comrades, and, on occasion, themselves. They bite on principle, and are functionally piranha with extra steps.

items.name: Titanstring Bow
items.description: Can only be bought if you successfully finish Find the Missing Shipment .

tooltip.lore: The commonly held belief that archers are rangey, thin-wristed people is completely false. It takes years of training to pull a bowstring all the way back to your cheekbone, and wrists like iron.
Useful for bank vaults, locked chests, and bolted barns.
The point of the rapier is capable of splitting into heated barbs, should the wielder decide to prick themselves to activate them - sacrifice for reward.

Yrre the Sparkstruck got their nickname for both their obsession with lightning and the inventions they created, which had the habit of electrocuting both Yrre and any poor observers that got too close.
Legends say the Giantbreaker earned its name when an elven ranger singlehandedly defeated a score of hill giants. While the ranger's name is long forgotten, Giantbreaker's lives on.
This dented piece of black metal has a red shine to it, no matter the lighting. Faint screams echo through your mind whenever you touch it.
Likely fashioned in tribute to the extremely small, high-vaulting rascal who pillaged many a hen house - Lupperdiddle Swires.

items.name: Gloves of Archery
items.description: Solid gloves with bonus damage for Ranged builds using bows.

tooltip.lore: Banded in dark iron, these gloves are embossed with the brass likeness of an elf against a trio of arrows in flight.
Sturdy and well-worn, these boots make a snug fit on any wearer.

items.name: Returning Pike
items.description: The best, throwing weapon for throwing builds until Act 2 or Act 3.

tooltip.lore: A 'Crazy Wilbur' is a technique invented by the dwarvish Ticklebeard Clan. It involves getting your larger enemies to swallow your weapon, then calling it back to you - as messily as possible.
Bringer of that which it is.

items.name: The Whispering Promise
items.description: If was not obtained in Emerald Grove from Volo

tooltip.lore: A soft, soothing voice floats from this ring, reciting a prayer over and over.

items.name: Game of Chicken
items.description: If you previously spared the Owlbear Cub (Lv.1) you will be able to add it to your party if you win the game and persuade Krolla to give it up. It can be easier to win the game by using Speak with Animals

items.name: Recruit Owlbear Cub
items.description: You can recruit the Owlbear Cub either by winning Game of Chicken or after dealing with the goblins convince it to go to your camp.​

items.name: Crusher's Ring
items.description: You will have to kiss Crusher goblin's foot to obtain this ring or kill him.

tooltip.lore: Soft, loving whispers emanate from this shield. Though barely audible and spoken in some long-forgotten language, they make you feel safe.

items.name: Bow of Awareness
items.description: Great bow that can be used as a stat stick to improve initiative, even if you do not use ranged attacks.

tooltip.lore: This halfling bow requires a full draw for smaller folk, but serves as a quick off-hand for those of greater height.
Stained brown and reeking of iron, this greataxe once belonged to a killer so deranged, his name was magically scrubbed from all histories.
The fire engulfing this staff seems to feed off thin air, never once burning the dark wood.
Glaives
Prey often thinks it has escaped the worst of the predator's bloodthirst and fury - but there are always further torments to be meted out.

items.name: Participate in Abdirak's Ritual
items.description: Completing the ritual you will gain a permenant bonus: Loviatar's Love ​

tooltip.lore: More club than scourge, this weapon has a serrated edge made for drawing long, shallow cuts.
Though shiny and recently polished, dried blood still fills the deepest crevices of this axe.
The leather handle is smooth with use, and slick with fresh blood.

items.name: Rescue Volo
items.description: Complete Rescue Volo quest before Save The Refugees . As a reward you will receive Blazer of Benevolence .

tooltip.lore: Even though the embroidered velvet won't help against a sharp blade, wearing it fills you with self-assuredness.

items.name: Volo's Ersatz Eye
items.description:
Can be acquired after inviting Volo to your camp and asking him to remove your tadpole. The surgery will fail but Volo will replace the damaged eye.

With the new eye you will gain a permenant See Invisibility effect.​

tooltip.lore: The point of this spear is blackened and melted - the uneven metal is designed to catch deep in the wounds it creates.

items.name: Brand of the Absolute
items.description: Handle the evenet where Priestess Gut wants to give you the Brand of the Absolute.

tooltip.lore: Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.
Stained by blood, and gifted to only the most faithful of True Souls to help preserve their lives.
Blood is smeared across the shield's face, exalting wielders who bear the Absolute's mark.

items.name: Assassin's Touch
items.description: Received if Sazza is escorted back to the Goblin Camp during quest Save the Goblin Sazza from Minthara.

tooltip.lore: This long, slender blade fits perfectly between the ribs of any unsuspecting victim.

items.name: Decide the Fate of Minthara
items.description:
You will have to decide what happens to Minthara:

• Assist her with her plan. You will be able to recruit her as a companion in Act 2.

• Knock her out during Defeat the Goblins or during the attack on the Emerald Grove . This will allow to still recruit her in Act 2.

• Kill her. You will receive powerful loot:

• Xyanyde

• Boots of Striding

• Spidersilk Armour

• Minthara's Tadpole

tooltip.lore: Cast deep in the Underdark by duergar slaves, the head of this mace is engraved with spiderwebs. It is ice-cold to the touch.

items.name: Spidersilk Armour
items.description: The Best Light Armor in Act 1.

tooltip.lore: Tracings of glossy black spider-web mark this drow-made armour. It is supple, but strong - and made to blend in with the dark caves and crevices of the Underdark.
The metal of these greaves is comfortably warm against your shins, as though heated by your mere presence.

items.name: Minthara's Tadpole
items.description: Carried by Nightwarden Minthara (Lv. 6) .

tooltip.lore: Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.

items.name: The Watersparkers
items.description: Located inside a gilded chest.

tooltip.lore: An enormous storm was raging the first time Yrre the Sparkstruck left the Underdark. The deep gnome's interest in lightning became at once an all-consuming obsession, and they would spend the rest of their years striving to recreate that storm's power themselves.

items.name: Deal with Dror Ragzlin (Lv. 5)
items.description:
Part of Rescue the Druid Halsin the whole camp will turn hostile from this point.

Don't forget to loot the Faithbreaker , his key and Dror Ragzlin's Tadpole from him.

tooltip.lore: A hand-like brand stamped into the leather handle obscures the notches that would normally signify a hobgoblin's rank.
Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.

items.name: Loot Dror Ragzlin's Vault
items.description:
It contains a few unique items:

• Springstep Boots

• Amulet of Selûne's Chosen

• Gloves of the Growling Underdog

• Infernal Iron

tooltip.lore: Supple leather rebounds with every step, as if urging the wearer on.
The gems and pearls of this necklace have a brilliant shine to them, as if reflecting unseen moonlight.

items.name: Gloves of the Growling Underdog
items.description: Very good gloves, specifically for martials that engage in close combat. The attack roll Advantage allows them to greatly improve their average damage.

tooltip.lore: Crowded taprooms. Packed feast halls. Bustling market bazaars. Chances to be social? Sure. Chances to be violent? Absolutely.
This dented piece of black metal has a red shine to it, no matter the lighting. Faint screams echo through your mind whenever you touch it.
The ring's emerald glows a deep, deadly green.

items.name: Defeat the Goblins
items.description: After exploring the whole camp including the below listed areas ( Defiled Temple and Worg Pens ) complete Defeat the Goblins quest.

tooltip.lore: 'No goblin will strike you down' is etched into this dagger's handle. When goblin assailants realised they couldn't kill the dagger's original owner, they gave him to the worgs, who made short work of him.
The green jewel set into this necklace's pendant is carved to resemble a cat's slivered pupil.
Lightweight fur and leather, designed to speed the wearer's charge into battle.

items.name: Deal With Polma
items.description: You can either deal with her peacefuly or defeat her in battle.

items.name: Amulet of Misty Step
items.description: Great amulet, as it can be useful for almost any character. Having a safe movement spell can save you from dying or allow you to gain a better vantage point.

items.name: Handle the Druid Sanctuary Encounter
items.description:
Don't forget to loot:

• The Sparkle Hands

• Wood Woad Shield

tooltip.lore: 'Sometimes I just want to feel that spark,' Yrre the Sparkstruck said. 'Feel the lightning course through me.'

'And with her arm the dryad linked a man who had once been her lover, and once of flesh and bone. Now his flesh was bark, and his bone heartwood. In his eyes a feverish love still blazed like a thousand fireflies in a spring meadow.'

When Gandrel was seven, he was frightened badly by a vampire spawn during a public burning. Seized by the flames, the creature didn't shrivel up. It swelled and burst like a squeezed leech. Brains splattered the boy's shoes, grey-pink and pulpy. He would never forget it.

items.name: Investigate People in the Entrance Gallery
items.description: Find out what happened to the people trapped here and continue to talk with the door and gain further access to the tunnel.

items.name: Auntie Ethel's Hair
items.description: You acn obtain this powerful item if you reduce The Hag's HP below 20% and wait for her turn.

tooltip.lore: A menacing cackle emanates from this twisted charm. It whispers to bite, to maim, to kill...
A pale green eye, pierced by the twisting branches of a dead tree - the result of a foolhardy woman begging Auntie for a favour.
Live worms wriggle beneath the crumbling surface of this half-rotten staff. Merely touching it fills you with a wave of unbearable nausea and a strange longing, as if it's calling out for its like.

items.name: Mayrina (if saved from The Hag)
items.description: You will have a small interaction with Mayrina if you save her from the Hag.

tooltip.lore: Though heavily decorated and inlaid with rubies, the blade's sharp edge suggests its use isn't merely ornamental.
This heavy crossbow is fashioned from a curious, tensile metal that doesn't seem to be native to Faerûn.

items.name: Silver Sword of the Astral Plane

tooltip.lore: The fervour with which the githyanki express devotion to their queen is admirable to some, disquieting to others. When confronted with the fallibility of the divine (since no one is wholly perfect), who knows what reaction such folk might evince.

items.name: Defeat the Minotaur (Lv. 4)
items.description: The Minotaur can be found in one of two locations. You can loot Amulet of the Unworthy off of it.

tooltip.lore: Once part of the Girdle of Blades, this buckle was torn off and fashioned into an amulet that can be worn by creatures of all sizes.

items.name: Defeat the Bulette (Lv. 5)
items.description:
Can appear in multiple locations.

You will be able to loot the Bloodguzzler Garb .

tooltip.lore: No sooner does blood speck the armour than it's absorbed, as if sustaining something within.

items.name: Phalar Aluve
items.description:
Stuck inside a rock

Extremely powerful weapon that can be used to empower your party member damage and other stats.

tooltip.lore: The drow inscription on this blade appears to have been recently carved. It translates to: 'Though I have to leave you, I will dance forever in Eilistraee's light.'

items.name: Investigate the Petrified Drow
items.description:
You will find some important loot including a quest item:

• Icy Helve

• The Blast Pendant

• Frayed Drow Hood

items.name: Icy Helve
items.description: Is one of 3 parts that are used to craft Mourning Frost

tooltip.lore: The ice-cold handle of what must once have been a staff. A single screw sticks out from the top.

Though they only worked together for a short time, it is hard to overstate the influence Lenore the cleric of Mystra had on Yrre the Sparkstruck, who often likened harnessing lightning to manipulating the Weave.

items.name: Frayed Drow Hood
items.description: Although this may not seem as very powerful item, it can be given to Gale , without sacrificing other good unique gear.

tooltip.lore: A supple leather cowl. Its spider web embroidery is stained, and pieces of leather and thread are slowly disintegrating.

items.name: Defeat Spectator (Lv. 5)
items.description: Will drop The Spectator Eyes .

tooltip.lore: Spectators are hideous aberrations that float above the ground, fiercely staring around and shooting beams at anything they don't appreciate.
Tracings of glossy black spider-web mark this drow-made armour. It is supple, but strong - and made to blend in with the dark caves and crevices of the Underdark.
A radiant and remarkably preserved ring, forged from arandur metal.

items.name: Defeat the Hook Horrors (Lv. 4) and Filro the Forgotten (Lv. 5)
items.description: You will be able to loot the Icy Crystal from Filro the Forgotten .

items.name: Icy Crystal
items.description: Is one of 3 parts that are used to craft Mourning Frost

tooltip.lore: This crystal is freezing to the touch, no matter how long you hold it. Shallow grooves run along the bottom, as if it used to be attached to something.

items.name: Sussur Tree Bark
items.description: Required item for Finish the Masterwork Weapon quest.

tooltip.lore: A piece of tough bark from a sussur tree. Holding it leaves a dull tingling in your fingers.
The metal bolted to the helmet's forehead is engraved with a faded script. Though the words have become unintelligible, their magic remains.
A set of runes has been expertly worked into the helmet's filigree. Almost imperceptible - but powerful nonetheless.

items.name: Luminous Armour
items.description: One of the best Cleric armor in the game.

tooltip.lore: Many an adventurer has found themselves swallowed up by the dark throat of some nameless dungeon, aching for a glimpse of light.
The soles of these boots warp and shift like warm clay manipulated by an unseen sculptor's hands.
This delicate circlet grants its wearer the ability to cast the Scorching Ray spell.

The wizard Otiluke wrote about bound weapons and their propensity to coalesce with their wielder as the pair come together. He also wrote about their dangers, which are manifold, and their benefits, which make the danger worth it.
Despite the many scorch marks, these leather boots are somehow still supple and intact.

items.name: Melf's First Staff
items.description: The best caster weapon in Act 1, can easily be used till Act 3

tooltip.lore: In spite of (or perhaps because of) his suspiciously amusing name, the wizard Melf was actually quite a talented spellcaster and innovator in the field of the arcane. He even led an order called the Knights of Luna.

items.name: Psychic Spark
items.description: Relevant amulet for any caster that greatly uses the Magic Missile ​ spell.

tooltip.lore: Every gem hanging from this necklace is warm to the touch, as if waiting to ignite.
The ring's arcane jewel parts all but the darkest of shadows.
'Lightning is like magic,' Yrre the Sparkstruck once said. 'And what is magic but life itself?'
This staff pulses with energy, swiftly attuning itself to the beat of your heart.
This ring is forged from a smooth, alien metal, capped with eyes of emerald that watch the world unblinking.

items.name: Boots of Stormy Clamour
items.description: It's one of the best boots in the game for any build that applies status effects or uses control spells.

tooltip.lore: Peals of thunder rumble at the back of your skull with every step taken in these boots.

items.name: Pearl of Power Amulet
items.description: Great amulet to restore a spell slot when needed. It can be unequipped after use.

tooltip.lore: There are no molluscs of power, alas.
A hymn to Eilistraee is inscribed along the inside of this ring, which is surprisingly heavy when worn.
One of the relics of Deep Duerra's rebellious children. Flanked by her court, Duerra shook her head at her children. Had they really believed they could leave her?
The gemstone centrepiece of the amulet was long ago mottled and dyed by the tears of a martyr.
Acid was long ago abandoned by assassins, who found poison much more reliable in spiking their targets' drinks. The acid kept melting the chalices.

items.name: Caustic Band
items.description: The ring is useful throughout the whole game due to its consistent and easy-to-apply damage rider.

tooltip.lore: Adjusting this unassuming ring on your finger, you hear a faint sizzle, as of pressure being released.

items.name: Gloves of Uninhibited Kushigo
items.description: Excellent throwing gloves that are useful till Act 3.

tooltip.lore: Forsaking his infamous armour, Kushigo left Irishi's body and wandered naked, without thought or care, into the barren countryside. The land was flat and desolate under the red-gold sun, but compared to his lonely heart, it seemed alive with joy.
The leather smells faintly of herbs, as if washed in a herbal bath.
The ring is scuffed and twisted out of shape, as if worn by big stony fingers. The owner of those fingers has carved 'Karthol' into the warped metal in a fit of affection or possessiveness.

items.name: Defeat the Duergar Intruders
items.description:
As a reward you will gain access to the locked room with some important loot:

• Icy Metal

• Shadow of Menzoberranzan

• "The Great Furnace of Grymforge" - book, that is used to start The Adamantine Forge

items.name: Icy Metal
items.description: Is one of 3 parts that are used to craft Mourning Frost

tooltip.lore: Specks of frost cover the hole in the bottom of this chunky piece of iron, where it was clearly screwed onto something.
You sense the shadows that envelop this drowcraft cowl, eagerly waiting to envelop you and shield you from view.

items.name: Cure Thulla
items.description: Cure Thulla to receive Boots of Speed

tooltip.lore: Featherlaced and flexible of sole, these boots have been used to round up - and outrun - many a foe.

items.name: Help Omeluum Investigate the Parasite
items.description:
After completing the quest you will be able to buy from Omeluum the following items:

• Pearl of Power Amulet

• Ring of Salving

• The Shadespell Circlet

• Boots of Stormy Clamour

• Creation's Echo

You will also be able to receive Ring of Mind-Shielding

items.name: Find the Mushroom Picker
items.description: You will receive Gloves of Uninhibited Kushigo as a reward.

items.name: Deliver Nere's Head
items.description:
Depending to who you deliver the head reward will be different:

• Envoy's Amulet - if you side with Sovereign Spaw

• Champion's Chain - if you side with Sovereign Glut

tooltip.lore: A fleshy amulet shaped by Sovereign Spaw's own hand. Wherever the bearer goes, so does the myconid circle.
A plump mass torn from Sovereign Glut's own flesh. A marker of the colony's esteem - or perhaps its property.

items.name: Avenge Glut's Circle
items.description:
Depending with who you side with, you will receive different rewards:

• Siding with Glut:

• Winter's Clutches

• If Siding with Spaw, you will be able to loot Glut:

• Hastening Spores

• Lump of Myconid Flesh

• Poison Spore

• Scroll of Blur

• Scroll of Hold Person

• Swarming Toadstool

tooltip.lore: Likely these originated in Icewind Dale, where tempers run as hot as hearths, and frozen rivers trace the arm of the dale in a network of blue-white tributaries.

items.name: Handle the Duergar Ambush
items.description: If you decide to fight them you will be able to loot the Exterminator's Axe .

tooltip.lore: The intricate engravings on this axe's blade and handle make plain the skill of its smith.

items.name: Shortsword of First Blood
items.description: Found on an executed Deep Gnome.

tooltip.lore: A golden blade, exquisitely made and astonishingly sharp.

items.name: Deal with the Arcane Turrets
items.description: It is possible to either defeat them or bypass them.

tooltip.lore: And they shall have Celestia's light, even if we must crack wide the heavens to bring it."

items.name: Loot the Chest of the Mundane
items.description: To get some mundane items.

tooltip.lore: Wearing these boots is like walking on cotton clouds.

items.name: Orpheus, Prince of the Comet, Part One: Betrayal
items.description: Take and interact with the githyanki slate in the third floor or bring it to Lae'zel.

tooltip.lore: Though carefully stowed away in a bedside chest, this ring is covered in dust and cobwebs.
Beneath the layers of dust covering this necklace, its inlaid gems still give off a blue sparkle - the colour of Mystra's clergy.

items.name: Deal with Bernard (Lv. 4)
items.description:
You can either have a peaceful resolution or defeat him in battle. For the peaceful resolution read The Roads of Darkness before encountering him.

You will be able to loot Light of Creation and Guiding Light (can be also acquired peacefully; required to access the Arcane Tower Basement ).

tooltip.lore: Small lodestones have been inserted along the halberd's shaft, a strange blue current running through them.

items.name: Guiding Light
items.description: Used to unlock the secret basement in the tower.

tooltip.lore: Used by the previous owner to access both the Underdark's darkest corners and her own basement, this ring seems to reflect light where there should be none.

items.name: Club of Hill Giant Strength
items.description: Check the stool at the top of the tower, it is easy to miss. By breaking it, you can get the item.

tooltip.lore: Though this rotting chair leg doesn't look like much, every splinter vibrates with barely-contained power.
Though dust has settled into every nook and cranny of this staff, it still emanates a soothing aura. Its previous owner cast it aside - forgotten because there was nobody to bless.

When the inventor Yrre met the cleric Lenore, sparks flew. Apart, they were in a rut, but together, they inspired each other to push the boundaries of innovation and invention. This ring is just one of their many scientific triumphs.
Infused with the inconceivable might of the formidable BOOOAL!
Scored with slime and blood, it seems this chain mail shirt couldn't save its previous owner. Although it may help the next.
Strong castle-forged steel surmounts a grip of sturdy leather - this immense sword is capable of disembowelling giants in a single swipe.
The impression of an elven face screams silently from the finely curved bow - the image becomes even more disturbing when the bowstring snaps forward on release, emitting a blood-curdling wail.
Look no further than Scyllua Darkhope - fallen paladin and champion of Bane - to see how the tools of good can be turned to evil purpose.

items.name: Intransigent Warhammer
items.description: Acquired during the encounter before arriving to the Grymforge

tooltip.lore: The elegant design of this hammer belies its brutal, crushing power. This weapon does not give ground.

items.name: Return the Sergeants Boots
items.description:
You will have to choose one of these rewards:

• Bracing Band

• Armour of Uninhibited Kushigo

tooltip.lore: A common ring, simple and unadorned. Though there is a certain heft to it...

items.name: The Protecty Sparkswall
items.description: The best robe for casters in Act 1.

tooltip.lore: Yrre the Sparkstruck was raised in the Underdark, so they weren't surprised that power begets envy, and envy begets violence. They were prepared.
A series of motivational quotes have been enthusiastically removed from the embroidery of these gloves, as if in protest.
'Is there anything as beautiful as lightning striking all around you?' Yrre the Sparkstruck said, letting lightning strike all around them.

items.name: The Bullied Gnome
items.description: Either bully or protect the gnome name Skickpit.

tooltip.lore: The smooth, six-pointed star on this ring is beset with small Underdark gemstones, as befits a talisman of the god of mining and patron of deep gnomes.

items.name: Speak With Philomeen About the Runepowder
Decide the Fate of True Soul Nere
items.description: If you will want to complete Deliver Nere's Head you will have to kill Nere .

items.name: Disintegrating Night Walkers
items.description: One of the best boot options throughout the game. Amazingly, you can get them this early.

tooltip.lore: A blade of flawless craft. As it parts the air, you fancy you can hear a faint, whispering shriek...
Darkness seems to blossom from this ring, heightened by the menacing glare of the Absolute's symbol, though for the Branded this symbol holds the promise of power.
Featherlaced and flexible of sole, these boots have been used to round up - and outrun - many a foe.
With every blow the skullcap seems to compress, narrowing the vision of the wearer.
Countless hands have smoothened the haft and sharpened the blade. This is an ancestral weapon - perhaps dating back to the ancient digs that sundered the duergar from their dwarf-kin.

items.name: Get Infernal Alloy
items.description:
Speak with Stonemason Kith and pass three checks (Investigation, Perception and History DC 5-10).

For Bards only 1 check is required (either History or Deception).

tooltip.lore: Though well-preserved, the armour's gleam is somewhat muted - as if half-shadowed, even in brightest day.
Stamped with Shar's own sigil, this helm was worn by the Nightsinger's own crusaders. The darkness within seems deeper than night, somehow.
Worn by Shar's most loyal zealots, the mere sight of it strikes fear in the heart of even the bravest of souls.

items.name: Get Past the Rubble
items.description: Completely optional quest. This is one of the ways to the Adamantite Forge . You can also enter the forge by using Misty Step or Fly .​​​

items.name: Idol of Shar
items.description: You can gift it to Shadowheart . Grants one Nightsong point.

tooltip.lore: Carved from black marble, this effigy is as terrible and perfect as the Lady of Loss herself.

items.name: Longsword Mould
items.description: Found on the catwalk. Used to craft Adamantine Longsword at the Adamantite Forge .

tooltip.lore: Even in its bare outline, the longsword looks both regal and powerful.

items.name: Shield Mould
items.description: Found on a pile of rubble. Used to craft Adamantine Shield at the Adamantite Forge .

tooltip.lore: As hefty as the mould seems, the shield it outlines promises to be all the more stalwart.
Sharp teeth grow out of the halberd's dark metal - if it is indeed metal at all.
Though the crossbow's bolts look burning hot, they are pleasantly warm to the touch.

items.name: Defeat the Ochre Jelly (Lv. 5)
items.description: Can either be reached from the lower level or upper. If trying to access from the upper level you can use Feather Fall to avoid getting damage.​

items.name: Scimitar Mould
items.description: Found on a skeleton. Used to craft Adamantine Scimitar at the Adamantite Forge .

tooltip.lore: Despite a dent in the mould's edge, the wicked shape of a scimitar is clear and well-defined.

items.name: Mace Mould
items.description: Used to craft Adamantine Mace at the Adamantite Forge .

tooltip.lore: The sharpened edges of a mace look deadly in this sturdy mould.

items.name: Scale Mail Mould (Medium)
items.description: Used to craft Adamantine Scale Mail at the Adamantite Forge .

tooltip.lore: The cycloid scales etched into this mould would be perfect for scale mail armour.

items.name: Splint Mould (Heavy)
items.description: Used to craft Adamantine Splint Armour .

tooltip.lore: Deep channels suggest a shell of thick metal bands - splint armour, proof against the fiercest of blows.

items.name: Mithral Ore
items.description: Used to craft Adamantine items.

tooltip.lore: Rough, unprocessed and raw - this mithral ore is ready to be fed into the forge.

items.name: Mithral Ore
items.description: Used to craft Adamantine items.

tooltip.lore: Rough, unprocessed and raw - this mithral ore is ready to be fed into the forge.

items.name: Help the Cursed Monk
items.description: Starts by taking the Sentient Amulet that is inside the Adamantine Chest . It can be unlocked with the Adamantine Key .

items.name: Sentient Amulet
items.description: One of the best amulets for Monk builds until Act 3.

tooltip.lore: Sun-graced warmth radiates from both the pendant and its chain. Somehow, even a brief glance of it brings a smile to your face.

items.name: Defeat Grym (Lv. 10)
items.description: Will drop Grymskull Helm

tooltip.lore: Once home to an inscrutable machine intelligence, Grym's skull now serves as a simple helm.

items.name: Adamantine Mace
items.description: Although the weapons might sound impressive, compared to other options in Act 1, they underperform, and it's better to get armor or a shield.

tooltip.lore: A nimble mace, heavy with the promise of broken bones.

items.name: Adamantine Scimitar
items.description: Although the weapons might sound impressive, compared to other options in Act 1, they underperform, and it's better to get armor or a shield.

tooltip.lore: Each cut of this blade shines with the wicked glint of adamantine - a promise for a devastating blow.

items.name: Adamantine Longsword
items.description: Although the weapons might sound impressive, compared to other options in Act 1, they underperform, and it's better to get armor or a shield.

tooltip.lore: Though heavy, this longsword swings effortlessly in your hand - ready to take down a hundred enemies.
The adamantine scales hug your body like a second skin - a heavy, near-impregnable skin.
The dull shine of the adamantine promises this shield could take a thousand beatings - and still look as good as new.
The adamantine plates lock and slide together perfectly - offering protection against even the deadliest of blades.
It was through scrying and observing blazing elementals that the monks of Plumgully Temple mastered the art of fighting fire with fists, and vice versa.

Carved in circles across the knob of the staff are different words for thunder. Gnomish, Sylvan, Elvish, Celestial, even the Primordial tongue - all of these and more fuse together to form the thunderword.
The slightest movement of leather makes a dark energy seep out of the gloves.

items.name: Winter's Clutches
items.description: If not obtained from Sovereign Glut in Ebonlake Grotto.

tooltip.lore: Likely these originated in Icewind Dale, where tempers run as hot as hearths, and frozen rivers trace the arm of the dale in a network of blue-white tributaries.
Toughened metal boots, highly insulated with heavy leather and padding for use in any and all elements.

Not the most elegant of weapons, the warpick's cumbersome weight certainly allows it to be deadly in combat, when wielded by a strong arm. Inexplicably, this one has a small rabbit carved into the handle.
Sometimes the best thing you can do for your friends is to stop bleeding and give them a chance to sort everything out.

items.name: Check Your Quests Before Entering the Rosymorn Monastery Tail
items.description: Double check if you have done quests you wanted as certain quests will be closed if you leave to the trail (e.g. Rescue the Druid Halsin )

items.name: Avoid Going to Shadow-Cursed Lands
items.description: For now avoid going to the Shadow-Cursed Lands as it will finish Act 1 and start Act 2.

items.name: Defeat the Undead Army (Lv. 3-6)
items.description: You can use Speak with Dead on the corpse around them.​

items.name: Steal a Githyanki Egg
items.description: You can either steal the Githyanki Egg from the hatchery in Crèche Y'llek or bring an Owlbear Egg from the Owlbear Nest

items.name: Talk With the Blue Jay
items.description: Use the spell Speak with Animals to talk with the Blue Jay.​

items.name: Find the Blood of Lathander
items.description:
In this part of the quest you will need to bring these item to the specified tables:

• Ceremonial Battleaxe - place it on the Dawnmaster Vaseid table.

• Ceremonial Mace - place it on the Dawnmaster Stockhold table.

• Ceremonial Warhammer - place it on the Dawnmaster Seed table.

After you complete the puzzle you you will obtain a note which you have to read to progress the quest and Dawnmaster's Crest .

items.name: Dawnmaster's Crest
items.description: Obtained after completing the above mentioned puzzle.

tooltip.lore: This unusually shaped crest bears the markings of a Lathanderian high priest, but its practical function is unclear.

items.name: Deal With the Giant Eagles
items.description: You can either deal with them peacefully or defeat them in combat.

items.name: Holy Lance Helm
items.description: Excellent helmet to be used together with Gloves of Belligerent Skies for additional Prone status stacks.

tooltip.lore: Brief halos of divine light wrap your head when you don and doff this helmet.
The sting of fear at the encroachment of some hulking brute is lessened by these gloves. In fact, in such moments, they send rivulets of courage coursing through you.

items.name: Gloves of Dexterity
items.description: These gloves can serve as an improvement to get higher Dexterity and improve attack rolls.

tooltip.lore: The point at which the gloves meet the wrist is form-fitting and very snug; almost a hint at the supple quickness and skill these gloves permit the wearer.
This flail was created to commemorate victory over the devil Belhifet. It has since made its way so far from Easthaven that the locals have long forgotten that chapter of their town's past.

items.name: Knife of the Undermountain King
items.description: Highly in-demand item that is perfect as a stat stick for many ranged builds.

tooltip.lore: Snatched from the realm of the mad wizard Halaster Blackcloak, this knife became a prized keepsake of a duergar king.
Tenser's notes on arcane displacement were not only read by wizards - other avid readers included enterprising smiths eager to break into the magic items market.
Deceptively light, the blade rasps through castle-forged plate as easily as Corellon Larethian's own anarchic holy sword through the hearts of evil orcs.
Yes, but what exactly is hitting you?
Thou shalt not suffer a witch to... well, keep doing whatever she's doing. It's probably malefic, involving toads and... and things.
Though thin and delicate-looking, the amulet's pendant sits heavy against your chest.

items.name: Varsh Ko'kuu's Boots
items.description: Can either be acquired by convincing Varsh Ko'kuu that you will save the Githyanki Egg or you can loot it from him.

tooltip.lore: Though the leather is slick with acid, the corrosive liquid doesn't seem to damage the boots - nor does it penetrate.

items.name: Githyanki Egg
items.description: Steal the egg to bring it to Lady Esther , her employers later in Act 3 or to Lae'zel .

tooltip.lore: A large egg with a soft, almost see-through shell, protected with iron bands. A githyanki child stirs within.

items.name: Obtain the Second Githyanki Disc
items.description: The second part is carried by Youth Varrl you can decide how you want to acquire the disc.

items.name: Explore Crèche Y'llek
items.description: Before continuing, explore and trade.

items.name: Talk With Ghustil Stornugoss
items.description:
She will attempt to remove the Illithid Tadpole from your head.

You can gain Awakened if you manage to win 3 DC saving throws:

• DC 12 Intelligence

• DC 15 Wisdom

• DC 18 Constitution/Wisdom/Charisma/Intelligence depending on your class.

items.name: Take Mind Flayer Parasite Specimens
items.description: You can find 3 Illithid Tadpoles in the Doctor's Office.

tooltip.lore: Many a githyanki hunter has worn this amulet before you. The strange stones set in swirling silver reverberate with echoes of their magic.
The mysterious gem set in this ring seems to change hues whenever you aren't looking.

items.name: Gain Access to the Inquisitors Chamber
items.description: You will either need to defeat Kith'rak Therezzyn or tell her that you have the Mysterious Artefact .

tooltip.lore: The sharp metal whispers to you. Pleading. Cursing... Echoes of slain foes.

items.name: Collect Loot at the Inquisitors Chamber

tooltip.lore: You get the eerie feeling that the jewelsmith used more than alien metals to forge this circlet - something like ancient magic, or shards of a soul.

items.name: Strange Conduit Ring
items.description: Excellent ring providing an easy psychic damage rider.

tooltip.lore: In his speculative writing on arcane displacement, the wizard Tenser notes the possibility for keenly focused minds to maintain spells, and even unlock hidden potential while doing so.

items.name: Diadem of Arcane Synergy
items.description: One of the best headwear for Paladin builds .

tooltip.lore: This circlet shimmers with a hundred otherworldly metals.

items.name: Gloves of Belligerent Skies
items.description: Excellent gloves that work well with builds aiming to control enemies via various damage effects.

tooltip.lore: Balling your fingers into fists while wearing these gloves causes a sonorous chime, like the fusion of a tempest and a church bell, to resound inside your skull.
The tourmaline in this necklace is almost uncomfortably hot, warmed by a heat all its own.
In mellow light, the halberd's curved steel head eerily resembles a grin.

items.name: Complete Find the Blood of Lathander
items.description:
If you have acquired the Dawnmaster's Crest this is going to be easier.

You will need to solve the Hidden Door Puzzle .

After approaching the altar the party has to place the Dawnmaster's Crest on it and the party can safely take Blood of Lathander .

If the party doesn't have the crest will have to risk the whole monastery being destroyed. To avoid this when the event starts you have to destroy the four Lathander Solar Machines .

tooltip.lore: The large chunk of amber set into the handle of this luminous mace supposedly holds four drops of godly blood, fallen to the earth in a battle between an avatar of Lathander and a maddened Chosen of Mystra.
A smattering of tiny ice crystals have formed cleats on the soles of these hardy boots.
The soft hum of power emanates from the alien metal.

items.name: Ring of Arcane Synergy
items.description: The ring has some niche uses with Martial builds.

tooltip.lore: The sharp silver corners of this ring almost cut into the stone set in its centre - a gem of which you've never seen the like before.

items.name: Speak with Kith'rak Voss
items.description: Speak with Kith'rak Voss at you camp the first long rest after you are done and left Crèche Y'llek .

items.name: Voss' Silver Sword
items.description: It can be obtained from Voss, if you decide to kill him.

tooltip.lore: Heavy and grouted in the gore of countless centuries, this prototype for the contemporary githyanki silver sword was wielded during their rebellion against the mind flayers who enslaved them.
